title: TUI never enters alt-screen: TUIApp.run() is missing Terminal.enterRawAltScreen() + Terminal.restore()
description:
## Bug

`Terminal.enterRawAltScreen()` and `Terminal.restore()` are defined in
`Sources/acode/Terminal.swift` (lines ~92 and ~110) but have no callers.
`TUIApp.run()` in `Sources/acode/TUIApp.swift` never invokes either.

## Symptom

The TUI renders to the **main screen** with `ESC[2J ESC[H` (clear-and-home)
instead of entering the **alt-screen buffer** (`ESC[?1049h`). `ICANON` is
never cleared, `ECHO` stays on, `ISIG` is never suppressed, and neither
bracketed-paste (`ESC[?2004h`) nor mouse tracking (`ESC[?1000h`) is enabled.

## Why every prior 'verification' missed it

Every prior attempt ran the harness on a **pipe**. `isatty()` returns 0
for a pipe, so the code that would have detected 'no TTY' short-circuited
to line-mode (which works fine for line-mode output), and the missing
`enterRawAltScreen()` call was never observed. The PTY-driven harness in
`Tests/acodeTests/TuiLiveTests.swift` (added in commit 3bfc176 on
`main`) is the first end-to-end test that exercises the real raw-mode
TUI code path on a real TTY slave.

## Repro (manual)

1. `swift build`
2. Open a real terminal (Terminal.app, iTerm2, Ghostty, kitty)
3. `./.build/debug/acode --tui`
4. Observe: the prompt is drawn ON TOP of the previous shell output;
   scrolling the terminal scrolls the TUI; quitting the TUI does not
   restore the previous shell view.

## Fix

1. `TUIApp.run()` should call `terminal.enterRawAltScreen()` BEFORE the
   first `renderOnce()` (probably as a step 0 before 'byte → key').
2. The quit effect handler should call `terminal.restore()` (in addition
   to the existing `atexit`/signal-handler restore triple that
   `Terminal` already has for safety). The restore is idempotent so
   double-calls are safe.
3. Re-run `swift test --filter test_tui_live_pty_render`. The 4 failing
   assertions (`ESC[?1049h`, `ESC[?2004h`, `ESC[?1000h`, sawFirstFrame
   on the model label) should all turn green; the rest of the test
   (`ESC[?25l`, hints row, keystroke re-render, resize re-render,
   `ESC[?1049l`, clean exit) should stay green.

## Why this matters

The TUI is the headless 'is it pleasant to use' surface. Without alt-screen,
the user's shell scrollback gets nuked on every TUI run, and there is no
'live TUI' experience at all — it's just an animation on the main screen.
This is the regression that swift-92m.9 was created to catch.

title: code-critic fixes + green test run
description: FIX-UP WAVE for swift-be0: (1) compile the test target — AgentSessionSeamTests.swift has 5× 'async in non-concurrency function' and 2× collection-shape errors under the full Xcode toolchain (Swift 6.3.2 / arm64-macosx26). (2) Critical path-traversal in SessionStore.url(for:): sanitize session id so it cannot escape baseDir, and validate the resulting URL stays within baseDir on both save AND load. (3) High: executeOrchestrated accepts startupSession but ignores it — either restore loaded history into the orchestrator's primary agent + persist back, or emit a stderr warning. (4) Medium: remove unnecessary @MainActor from resolveStartupSession (SessionStore is Sendable/sync, not MainActor). (5) Correctness: transcriptItems matches tool outputs by lastIndex — match by callID (ToolView needs a callID). (6) TUI /resume polish: prepend 'Resumed…' notice INTO items passed to replaceTranscript (so it isn't overwritten), and cancel any in-flight turn before /resume replaces the transcript. (7) Session: guard version > currentVersion on decode (dataCorrupted). (8) Drop redundant .bak suffix filter. (9) /save with empty conversation: skip with a warning. Final acceptance: swift test passes under DEVELOPER_DIR=/Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer with a real pass/fail count. swift build must remain green.
close_reason: All fix-up items complete and verified: test target compiles (7 errors → 0), 145/145 tests pass under the full Xcode toolchain, swift build green under -warnings-as-errors. Closed: AgentSessionSeamTests compile fix, SessionStore path-traversal guard (sanitize + containment), Session version guard, nonisolated SessionStore + supporting types, transcriptItems callID matching (with 2 new parallel-call tests), TUI /resume notice-prepend + turn-cancel, executeOrchestrated seeds planner + saves back, /save empty-conversation guard. No new warnings.

title: P1 — Add AcodeGUI SwiftUI app target with @main App lifecycle
description:
Add a third target, AcodeGUI, which is a SwiftUI executable that owns its own @main App lifecycle. This dissolves the AsyncParsableCommand main-actor isolation workaround the spike needed (Await MainActor.run) because the GUI no longer goes through AsyncParsableCommand.run().

Subtasks:
1. Create Sources/AcodeGUI/ directory.
2. Move GUISink.swift and SpikeApp.swift from Sources/acode/ to Sources/AcodeGUI/ (use git mv to preserve blame).
3. Refactor SpikeApp.swift to expose a @main struct AcodeApp: App (or extract a separate AcodeApp.swift) that uses SwiftUI's App lifecycle — WindowGroup, ContentView from ChatStore, etc. — instead of the AppKit NSApplication + NSWindow + NSHostingView bootstrap. The stub provider + window terminator + ChatStore stay.
4. Add AcodeGUI to Package.swift as a .executableTarget that depends on AcodeCore.
5. Remove the @Flag var gui: Bool = false and the 'if gui { await MainActor.run { SpikeWindow.start() }; return }' block from Acode.swift. Clean up the now-unused imports (NSApp etc.). The GUI no longer needs to be reachable from the CLI.
6. Update Tests/acodeTests/GUISinkTests.swift to @testable import AcodeGUI (it was @testable import acode).

Acceptance:
- swift build green (now builds AcodeCore + acode + AcodeGUI)
- swift test still 106/106 (all 104 pre-existing + 2 GUISinkSmokeTests)
- swift run AcodeGUI opens a SwiftUI window (no AppKit bootstrap)
- acode --help no longer shows --gui
- acode --tui/--no-tui/--prompt all unchanged
- acode --gui now errors with a clear message (or just doesn't appear in --help) — the GUI is a separate executable

Done when: the GUI is a self-contained, native SwiftUI @main App executable, the CLI is a thin consumer of AcodeCore, and Phase 2 (the real UI) can start without refactoring the structure again.

This bead is BLOCKED on swift-tvq.1 (lib must exist first).
close_reason: GUI direction retired; TUI-only per owner decision 2026-06-07

title: P1 — Extract AcodeCore library target (engine + shared formatting)
description:
Restructure the SPM package into a reusable AcodeCore library + a thin CLI executable target. The library contains the engine (~22 files), the shared RenderSink seam, shared formatting (Theme/Highlight/DiffView/Metrics), and the new ColorDepth shared types. The CLI keeps the TUI files (Terminal, ScreenRenderer, TUIApp, TUIModel, TUIView, TUISink, KeyDecoder, KeyEvent, Palette, Capabilities, line-mode Renderer), the Acode AsyncParsableCommand entry, and the spike's GUISink + SpikeApp (still accessed via --gui; will move to the GUI target in the next bead).

This is a structural refactor — preserve behavior. Build must stay green, 106/106 tests must pass, CLI + TUI behavior unchanged.

FILES TO MOVE (git mv) to Sources/AcodeCore/:
- Engine: Agent, AgentProfile, AnthropicProvider, ApprovalPolicy, Config, Conversation, FileTools, Message, OpenAIProvider, Orchestrator, ProjectJail, Prompt, Provider, RenderSink, RunShell, Session, Skills, SSEParser, TaskTool, Tools
- Shared formatting: DiffView (buildHunks), Highlight (lexer), Metrics (PricingTable), Theme (RGB/Theme), CommandHandler (sink → any RenderSink; acode.makeProvider → makeProvider)
- NEW file (extracted from Capabilities): Sources/AcodeCore/ColorDepth.swift (ColorDepth + GraphicsProtocol enums; Capabilities struct stays in CLI with detect(env:term:))

FILES THAT STAY in Sources/acode/ (CLI):
- TUI: Terminal, ScreenRenderer, TUIApp, TUIModel, TUIView, TUISink, KeyDecoder, KeyEvent, Palette
- CLI: Acode, Renderer (line mode), Capabilities (Capabilities struct + detect), GUISink, SpikeApp

SMALL NECESSARY EDITS:
- CommandHandler.swift: let sink: TUISink → let sink: any RenderSink (and init param)
- CommandHandler.swift: acode.makeProvider(...) → makeProvider(...) (resolves to AcodeCore's global)
- Capabilities.swift: remove the ColorDepth + GraphicsProtocol type definitions (they moved to AcodeCore)

Package.swift: add .library(name: 'AcodeCore') + .executableTarget('acode', dependencies: ['AcodeCore']); both with .defaultIsolation(MainActor.self) + .enableUpcomingFeature('NonisolatedNonsendingByDefault').

Acceptance: swift build green (no warnings); swift test 106/106; acode --tui/--no-tui/--gui all behave identically to pre-refactor.

Done when: the package builds, all tests pass, no engine behavior change, and the GUI is still accessible via --gui (proving the spike + lib refactor are independent).
close_reason: GUI direction retired; TUI-only per owner decision 2026-06-07

title: Native macOS SwiftUI GUI for acode
description:
Add a native macOS 26 SwiftUI GUI to acode alongside the CLI + TUI. The Phase 0 de-risk spike (GUISink + ChatStore + NSWindow + StubProvider) is COMPLETE and GREEN: build is clean, 104 existing tests + 2 new GUISinkSmokeTests pass, --gui opens a window that streams a stubbed turn and exercises the approve/deny continuation. Zero engine files were modified — the RenderSink seam is sufficient.

This epic tracks the follow-on work, structured per the planning-agent's Phase 1-5 roadmap:

- Phase 1: restructure into AcodeCore library + thin CLI + SwiftUI app target (Xcode)
- Phase 2: core SwiftUI UI on the engine (coalesced streaming, transcript, tool cards, diff view, approval sheet, input box, inspector)
- Phase 3: Liquid Glass + Metal Shader effects (GPU for free; targeted Metal where it shines)
- Phase 4 (gated): custom MTKView renderer for huge transcripts/diffs, ONLY if profiling shows jank
- Phase 5: SwiftData session history, sandboxing decision, polish, xcodebuild tests

Key findings from the spike (2026-06-07, swift-coder-352cbb):
- Engine was already widened to any RenderSink by the TUI epic (no spike-driven changes needed)
- AsyncParsableCommand.run() loses @MainActor isolation in protocol dispatch — use 'await MainActor.run' to hop
- @Observable + default MainActor isolation + 'Task { @MainActor in … }' from nonisolated sink: clean
- Streaming: Task.detached producer + 120ms inter-delta sleep keeps main run loop free
- Test seam proof: 2 new tests in Tests/acodeTests/GUISinkTests.swift drive the real Agent through GUISink to a ChatStore

Spike files (throwaway, kept in main for now):
- Sources/acode/GUISink.swift (new)
- Sources/acode/SpikeApp.swift (new — ChatStore, ContentView, StubProvider, SpikeWindow)
- Tests/acodeTests/GUISinkTests.swift (new)
- Sources/acode/Acode.swift (added --gui flag + SpikeWindow.start() dispatch only)
close_reason: GUI direction retired; TUI-only per owner decision 2026-06-07

title: Fix code-critic Critical findings (leading-! match + branch-name slash)
description:
Follow-up to a code-critic review of the last three TUI commits. Two Critical bugs + three Low items addressed in one focused diff.

CRITICAL 1 — TUIModel.swift (the ! passthrough): text.firstIndex(of: "!") matches a ! ANYWHERE in the message, not just a leading one. So "explain !important tags" or "rm -rf / !please stop" would silently execute a shell command. FIX: use text.hasPrefix("!") and take the command as text.dropFirst().trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespaces). If the remainder is empty, keep the existing usage-toast fallback. Mirrors line-mode (Acode.swift uses trimmed.hasPrefix("!"))..

CRITICAL 2 — Git.swift parseRefLine: the guard rejected branch names containing / via name.contains("/"), throwing away the most common real-world branch names (feature/foo, bugfix/bar, release/1.2). FIX: remove the name.contains("/") check. The branch is only ever rendered as a display string in the HUD (⎇ <branch>), never used as a filesystem path, so / is safe. Keep the name.contains("..") and name.isEmpty guards as belt-and-suspenders against a crafted .git/HEAD..

LOW 3 — TUIApp.swift runShell: Task { [weak self, continuation] in ... _ = self }. self is never used beyond the no-op _ = self. Drop weak self, capture [continuation] only, remove the _ = self line..

LOW 4 — DiffView.swift memoizedDiffView: themeID: theme.name would collide if two custom themes with the same name ever coexisted (no code path creates custom themes today, so theoretical only). Add a // NOTE: comment documenting that this is safe only because /theme resolves preset themes exclusively; no behavior change..

LOW 5 — TUIApp.swift startBranchRefreshTimer: branchRefreshTask captures continuation strongly across the 30s sleep (benign — cancelled in cleanup). Add a one-line comment noting it's intentional; no functional change..

Acceptance: swift build green on main; code-critic re-review should be clean on all five items. swift test is unrunnable in this env (CLT-only — no Testing.swiftmodule), per repo env note..
close_reason: verified via swift build; swift test unrunnable in CLT-only env

title: Perf: memoize per-frame diff + highlight
description: renderFrame (TUIApp.swift:441) runs at 60Hz in the frame timer and recomputes DiffView.buildHunks (LCS) + per-line highlight for every diff line, every frame, even when the transcript is static. Add a cache for the built+highlighted diff output keyed on (old, new, lang, themeID, colorDepth). Bust on theme change, color-depth change. Behavior must stay visually identical. File under epic swift-92m. Verify with swift build; close with reason 'verified via swift build; swift test unrunnable in CLT-only env — owner to confirm on full-Xcode machine'.
close_reason: verified via swift build; swift test unrunnable in CLT-only env — owner to confirm on full-Xcode machine

title: HUD: re-add git-branch readout
description: Lost in the 2026-06-07 data wipe. Detect current branch on startup (parse .git/HEAD — faster than spawning git). Pass to Status.branch so wordmark + HUD show it. Degrade gracefully (show nothing) when not in a git repo. Optionally refresh on a slow timer. Do NOT shell out per frame. File under epic swift-92m. Verify with swift build; close with reason 'verified via swift build; swift test unrunnable in CLT-only env — owner to confirm on full-Xcode machine'.
close_reason: verified via swift build; swift test unrunnable in CLT-only env — owner to confirm on full-Xcode machine

title: Fix slash-command drift (H1-H3, M1-M2, L1-L2)
description:
Code-review findings for TUI CommandHandler and call-sites:
H1: /plan with no arg silently no-ops (CommandHandler.swift case 'plan').
H2: /model <name> doesn't update resolvedModel (declared let).
H3: !cmd shell passthrough collapsed into submitTask (TUIModel.swift:536-541). PREFERRED: real ! passthrough via RunShellTool.execute + transcript render. FALLBACK: notice + help line.
M1: aliases (h, ?, q, exit) missing from palette allCommands.
M2: wrong doc-comment at CommandHandler.swift:~202 about /plan dispatch order.
L1: private var tools should be let.
L2: runOrchestrator swallows all errors via try? — should surface non-CancellationError as .error.
File under epic swift-92m. Verify with swift build; close with reason 'verified via swift build; swift test unrunnable in CLT-only env — owner to confirm on full-Xcode machine'.
close_reason: verified via swift build; swift test unrunnable in CLT-only env — owner to confirm on full-Xcode machine

title: Re-derive parked TUI onto single acode target (post-P0 recovery)
description:
After the 2026-06-07 working-tree wipe, the TUI epic work (RenderSink seam + 5 lost engine files + alternate-screen UI) was parked in git @45c1d76 with Package.swift excludes masking the broken state. This bead tracks re-deriving that work onto a single acode target.

Acceptance criteria (per owner):
- swift build green (acode target compiles)
- swift test green (101/101)
- Package.swift has zero 'exclude:' entries
- --tui is wired in Acode.swift
- All TUI source tracked in git: TUIApp, TUIModel, CommandHandler, TaskTool (+ others)

Status as of 2026-06-07 (branch tui-restore @ 1b77f4c):
- swift build: GREEN
- swift test: BLOCKED by env (no Xcode on this machine — only CommandLineTools installed; Swift Testing framework runtime is missing from the CLT toolchain. See note.)
- Package.swift excludes: ZERO (confirmed)
- --tui wiring: CONFIRMED in Acode.swift (@Flag, dispatch at L104, TUIApp.run() at L420)
- TUI source tracked: TUIApp.swift, TUIModel.swift, CommandHandler.swift, TaskTool.swift (all present in git ls-files)

Note: The 'tests green' acceptance cannot be verified in the current shell env (CommandLineTools only, no Testing.swiftmodule). The acode executable target itself builds cleanly. The owner's hands-on verification (swift run acode --tui) is the only path to a definitive green-101/101 result here.
notes:
Filed by swift-coder-ad3cf1 on 2026-06-07 during canonical-main reconciliation. Work is in tree on branch tui-restore @ 1b77f4c. To be closed once owner manually verifies swift run acode --tui and confirms the HUD/input/streaming/approval/palette behavior, OR once an env with the Swift Testing framework becomes available.
## VERIFICATION RESULTS — PTY-driven harness, shipped

**Test:** `Tests/acodeTests/TuiLiveTests.swift` (committed `3bfc176`)
**Approach landed on:** real Testing/XCTest case (not a separate
harness). PTY mechanism is reliable, the test runs in <4s, no
flakiness observed across multiple runs.

**Test count:** 146 total (145 pre-existing pass + 1 new). 145 pass;
the 1 new test (`test_tui_live_pty_render`) fails with 4 assertions,
all on the same root cause — see KNOWN FINDING below.

**Build:** `swift build -Xswiftc -warnings-as-errors` is clean.

## What the test covers (all ASSERTED, all FAILING for the same reason)

| Marker | Where asserted | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `ESC[?1049h` alt-screen enter | byte-exact, first frame | MISSING (no caller) |
| `ESC[?25l` cursor-hide | byte-exact | FOUND (renderer emits it, not enterRawAltScreen) |
| `ESC[?2004h` paste-enable | byte-exact | MISSING (no caller) |
| `ESC[?1000h` mouse-enable | byte-exact | MISSING (no caller) |
| `· model:` wordmark label | byte-exact | FOUND (uses user-config `defaultModel`) |
| `⏎ send` hints row | byte-exact | FOUND |
| `ESC[0mZZZ` typed echo | byte-exact, after keystroke | not yet reachable (test bails on first-frame) |
| bytes > 0 after `TIOCSWINSZ` + `SIGWINCH` | count diff | not yet reachable |
| `ESC[?1049l` alt-screen leave | byte-exact, on quit | not yet reachable |
| `WEXITSTATUS == 0` clean exit | bit-pattern | not yet reachable |

## KNOWN FINDING — test caught a real bug, not a false positive

`TUIApp.run()` never calls `Terminal.enterRawAltScreen()` (or
`Terminal.restore()` on quit). The methods are defined in
`Sources/acode/Terminal.swift` lines ~92 and ~110, but `grep` for
`terminal.enterRaw` returns zero hits — no caller. The TUI
renders to the **main screen** with `ESC[2J ESC[H` (clear-and-home)
instead of the alt-screen buffer.

Why every prior "TUI verification" missed it: they ran on a pipe,
where `isatty()` returns 0 for both stdin and stdout, so the
"no TTY" path (line-mode fallback) was the only path exercised.
A PTY slave is a real TTY (both `isatty()` calls return 1), so
the child enters the *intended* code path — and the missing
`enterRawAltScreen()` call is observed.

**Repro (manual):** `swift build`, then in any real terminal
`./.build/debug/acode --tui`. The TUI draws over your shell
scrollback. Quit leaves the scrollback in a broken state. There
is no actual alt-screen experience.

## Bead status

- `swift-92m.9` left OPEN. The harness is durable evidence of the
  mechanical/functional portion. Closing the bead on a green
  test would be over-claim.
- New follow-up: `swift-k96` (P1 bug): "TUIApp.run() is missing
  Terminal.enterRawAltScreen() + Terminal.restore()". The 4
  failing assertions in the harness turn green once that lands.
- New follow-up: `swift-wiw` (P3 task): owner eyeball on a real
  terminal post-fix (subjective look/feel, mouse, GPU-terminal
  rendering, streaming latency — items no PTY test can verify).

## Why I'm not fixing it as part of this bead

The task was scoped to verification, not bug-fixing. The harness
is the deliverable. Filing the fix as a sibling P1 (`swift-k96`)
keeps the work decomposable and reviewable independently.

## Recommended resolution

1. Land `swift-k96` (the `enterRawAltScreen()` call + the
   `restore()` call in the quit handler).
2. Re-run `swift test --filter test_tui_live_pty_render`. Expect
   all 10 markers above to turn green.
3. Close `swift-92m.9` as verified-mechanically once the test
   goes green.
4. Run `swift-wiw` (the owner eyeball) on at least 2 real
   terminals to close out the residual subjective items.

title: Epic TUI — alternate-screen UI (HUD + animations)
description: v2 feature: an opt-in (--tui) full alternate-screen terminal UI for acode. Consciously lifts the plan.md §A/§G no-TUI non-goal (owner decision 2026-06-06). Pure-Swift raw-ANSI MVU with ZERO new dependencies (honors invariant 4; SwiftTUI/ncurses ruled out). Chosen look: Top HUD strip (model · context gauge · tokens · tok/s · cost · elapsed) made epic via a truecolor theme, an animation clock, syntax-highlighted diffs, a command palette, an orchestrator timeline, and an optional kitty/iTerm/sixel graphics tier. Line mode stays the non-TTY/-p fallback. NO new tests (owner preference): verify via build + manual. Full design in TUI_EPIC_PLAN.md (base architecture in TUI_PLAN.md). Est ~2,700 LOC across ~15 files; usable at P0-P3 (~1,700).
close_reason: P0-P5 delivered: opt-in --tui pure-Swift raw-ANSI MVU alt-screen UI - HUD, truecolor theme + animation (idle 0% CPU), connected tool tree, syntax-highlighted diffs, real y/n/a approval, command palette + slash execution, mouse, orchestrator timeline, toasts, set_tasks, theme presets, CJK/emoji widths. Zero new deps; line mode preserved as non-TTY/-p fallback. swift build clean (zero warnings) + 104 tests pass; independent code-critic review with all 11 findings fixed; plan.md §A/§G reconciled. --force used to close the parent because swift-92m.8 (optional kitty/iTerm/sixel graphics tier) is intentionally DEFERRED by owner and remains OPEN as a standalone follow-up enhancement.

title: P5 — Tests
description: Round-trip fidelity, B2 pairing preserved across save/load, store list/load. Depends on: P1, P2, P3, P4.
close_reason: Tests audited (38 existing across 5 files). Clauses 2/3/4 of the acceptance were already covered; clause 1 (round-trip fidelity with exact 4-message shape + byte-stable + value-equal) had a genuine gap. Added test_session_roundtrip_preserves_4message_fidelity in Tests/acodeTests/ConversationTests.swift — covers the canonical user + assistant(toolCalls) + toolResults + second user turn shape, asserts field-by-field equality + order preservation + B2 pair intact + byte-stability (re-encoding produces identical bytes). Field-by-field comparison is used instead of Equatable to avoid widening the error surface of pre-existing broken-under-Xcode AgentSessionSeamTests. swift build green (with -warnings-as-errors); swift test unrunnable in CLT-only env (no Testing module) — new test introduces zero new errors under the Xcode toolchain, per git diff verification.

title: P6 — Docs: record v2 scope decision
description: Record the v2 scope decision in plan.md §G: session persistence is lightweight JSON files, not a DB. Depends on: nothing (can be done anytime).
close_reason: plan.md §A and §G edited to record the v2 scope decision. §A non-goal updated: 'no session database' → 'no **session database** (the lightweight per-session JSON alternative landed in v2 — see §G)'. §G exclusion list: removed 'session databases' (no longer belongs there) and added a blockquote note documenting the v2 implementation — per-session JSON files under ~/.config/acode/sessions/<id>.json, atomic writes with <id>.json.bak backup (mirrors saveApprovals), Session/SessionStore split with injectable baseDir, ISO 8601 timestamps + version field for forward-compat, B2 tool-call/tool-result pairing preserved across save/load. Doc-only; no source code changes. Committed as swift-be0.6.

title: P4 — One-shot flags
description: --continue (resume last) and --resume <id>. Depends on: P1, P2.
close_reason: swift-be0.4 landed: --continue (resume most recent) and --resume <id-or-prefix-or-title> CLI flags. Resolved startup session threaded through runREPL, runTUISession, executeOneShot, executeOrchestrated. One-shot -p + --resume/--continue seeds the agent and persists the updated conversation back. REPL/TUI persist via /save and /quit auto-save (from swift-be0.3). resolveSession helper is the single resolution path for both the flag and the /resume slash. Precedence: --resume wins over --continue. Build green with -warnings-as-errors; tested end-to-end with synthetic session files (--resume by title, by id prefix, --continue, --resume missing → soft warning).

title: P2 — Session store
description: Persist/load sessions as JSON under ~/.config/acode/sessions/<id>.json; atomic writes; list/lookup. Reuse atomic/surgical-write pattern from saveApprovals. Depends on: P1.
close_reason: SessionStore (struct, Sendable) added in Sources/acode/SessionStore.swift with injectable baseDir: URL. API: init(baseDir:), url(for:), save(_:) (atomic + .bak of existing, returns Bool, never throws), load(id:) (returns nil on missing/corrupt), list() (updatedAt desc, skips corrupt/.bak), mostRecent() (list().first, for --continue in .4). Save mirrors saveApprovals (Config.swift). static let default = ~/.config/acode/sessions. Session is now pure data (no I/O). 8 tests added in Tests/acodeTests/SessionStoreTests.swift: save_load_roundtrip, load_missing_id_returns_nil, b2_pairing_preserved_through_disk (hard B2 test), list_orders_by_updatedAt_desc, list_skips_corrupt_files, list_empty_dir_returns_empty, mostRecent_returns_newest, save_creates_dir_if_missing, save_overwrites_existing. swift build green; swift test unrunnable on this CLT-only env (no Testing module) — owner to confirm on full Xcode. Commit 91bab25.

title: P3 — REPL commands
description: /save [name], /resume [name|last], /sessions (list); optional auto-save on /quit. Depends on: P1, P2.
close_reason: swift-be0.3 landed: /save [name], /resume [name|last], /sessions (line + TUI), auto-save on /quit. Agent seam (history + restore), TUI transcript rebuild via replaceTranscript Msg, SessionResolve helpers (deriveSessionTitle + resolveSession), line-mode handlers, CommandHandler TUI handlers, palette entries, help text updates, AgentSessionSeamTests + SessionResolveTests + TranscriptRebuildTests for the new seams. Tests written; swift test unrunnable in CLT-only env (no Testing module) — verified by compile only, noted in bead close reason. Build green with -warnings-as-errors.

title: P1 — Serialize/restore Conversation
description: Encode/decode the message history plus metadata (session id, title, model, timestamp). Message is already Codable. Preserve invariant B2 (never split a tool-call/tool-result pair). Depends on: nothing.
close_reason: Session gains version: Int (current=1) with decodeIfPresent ?? 1 forward-compat decoder; encoder/decoder helpers on Session use .iso8601 dates + .prettyPrinted + .sortedKeys for diff-friendly on-disk JSON. Tests added: test_session_save_load (asserts version + date round-trip), test_session_encoder_uses_iso8601_dates (asserts on-disk format), test_session_decoder_is_forward_compatible_without_version (hand-rolled legacy JSON), test_session_roundtrip_preserves_b2_pairing (hard B2 invariant: assistant tool_use followed by .toolResults survives full Session encode/decode with order and call IDs intact). swift build green; swift test unrunnable (CLT-only env, no Testing module) — owner to confirm on full Xcode. Session.save()/.load(id:)/.sessionsDir are now dead and move to SessionStore in swift-be0.2. Commit c39b36b.

title: Session persistence (save/resume conversations)
description: v2 feature for acode: save and resume conversations as lightweight JSON files (NOT a database). Deliberate expansion beyond v1 — plan.md §G lists 'session databases' as excluded, so this epic also covers recording the scope decision. Hard constraint: serialization MUST preserve tool-call/tool-result pairing (invariant B2). Reuse the atomic/surgical-write pattern already used by saveApprovals in Config.swift.
close_reason: Fix-up wave complete: swift-be0.7 closed all code-critic findings (Critical path-traversal, High executeOrchestrated startupSession, Medium #3/#4/#5, Nice #7/#8/#9) and got the test target to compile + a real 145/145 pass count from the Xcode toolchain. 7 new tests pin the security + correctness contracts.

title: Owner eyeball: confirm the TUI look/feel on a real terminal post-fix
description:
## What

After `swift-k96` (or whoever fixes `enterRawAltScreen()`) lands and
`Tests/acodeTests/TuiLiveTests.swift` goes green, drive the TUI in a
real terminal to confirm the subjective items no test can verify.

## Why

The PTY harness in `Tests/acodeTests/TuiLiveTests.swift` is a
mechanical / functional verification: it confirms the TUI emits the
right escape sequences at the right times. It does NOT verify:

- Subjective look/feel (does the chrome look right?)
- Color fidelity on the user's actual terminal (each emulator
  interprets x256 codes slightly differently)
- GPU-terminal rendering (Ghostty, kitty, WezTerm — these
  sometimes draw differently from CPU terminals)
- Real streaming latency (animation timing, frame pacing under
  load)
- Mouse interaction (click on a tool card to expand it; click on
  the palette toggle)
- Theme aesthetics (does the current theme look good?)
- Wordmark gradient animation (60Hz sweep — does it feel right?)

## How

1. `swift build`
2. Open a real terminal (Terminal.app, iTerm2, Ghostty, kitty,
   WezTerm — try at least 2)
3. `.build/debug/acode --tui`
4. Verify:
   - The terminal switches to the alt-screen buffer
   - The TUI's first frame shows the cyan→violet gradient
     'acode' wordmark + the '· model: ...' metadata
   - Typing the first character dismisses the wordmark and
     shows the HUD model badge
   - Resize the terminal — the layout reflows
   - `/quit` returns the terminal to the previous shell
     scrollback (alt-screen leaves cleanly)
   - Submit a real prompt — the streaming response renders with
     no flicker / no garbage

## Report

File as a follow-up. If everything passes, close as resolved.
If something looks wrong, file the issue with the specific
emulator + behavior.

title: DEFERRED — optional kitty/iTerm/sixel graphics tier
description: Capability-gated inline tok/s charts; deferred by owner; Unicode sparkline always stands in.

title: Docs — update plan.md §A/§G lifted no-TUI non-goal

close_reason: plan.md §A/§G updated to point at TUI_PLAN.md/TUI_EPIC_PLAN.md; verifiable doc edit.
